front: Building: this Berlin museum features a zigzag plan, is accessible only through underground tunnels, and is connected to the Holocaust Tower.
Building: this Berlin museum contains sections called 'voids' representing the emptiness left by the erasures of the Holocaust.
Building: the Memory Void of this Berlin museum contains thousands of iron cutouts resembling human faces in Menashe Kadishman's 'Fallen Leaves' installation.
Building: the Garden of Exile of this Berlin museum features 49 pillars planted with oleaster.
Building: this Berlin museum's metal exterior is perforated by slash-mark-like windows.
Building: a staircase in this Berlin museum unexpectedly leads up to a blank wall.
Building: this architect won the competition to design the rebuilt World Trade Center site after September 11, 2001.
back: One World Trade Center / World Trade Center Masterplan (Daniel Libeskind)
work: One World Trade Center / World Trade Center Masterplan

front: Building: this architect's WTC design called for a building with a 1,776-foot-tall spire.
back: One World Trade Center / World Trade Center Masterplan (Daniel Libeskind)
work: One World Trade Center / World Trade Center Masterplan

front: Building: developer Larry Silverstein had David Childs of Skidmore, Owings and Merrill rework and largely discard this architect's original WTC design, which had been inspired by the Statue of Liberty.
back: One World Trade Center / World Trade Center Masterplan (Daniel Libeskind)
work: One World Trade Center / World Trade Center Masterplan

front: Building: this architect's original WTC plan called for the tower's shadow to create a 'wedge of light' illuminating Santiago Calatrava's neighboring transportation hub.
back: One World Trade Center / World Trade Center Masterplan (Daniel Libeskind)
work: One World Trade Center / World Trade Center Masterplan

front: Building: this extension of the Denver Art Museum has a jagged form inspired by the Rocky Mountains.
back: Frederic C. Hamilton Building (Denver Art Museum) (Daniel Libeskind)
work: Frederic C. Hamilton Building (Denver Art Museum)

front: Building: the Imperial War Museum North in Manchester, considered a key example of deconstructivism, was designed by this architect.
Building: the controversial Michael Lee-Chin 'Crystal' expansion of the Royal Ontario Museum in Toronto was designed by this architect.
Building: three triangular towers this architect designed for Rome, near AS Roma's planned stadium, were rejected in 2017.
Building: this architect designed 'The Curved One' skyscraper for Milan's CityLife project.
Building: this architect designed the Danish Jewish Museum in Copenhagen.
Architect: this Polish-American architect is associated with deconstructivism, a movement connected to Jacques Derrida's theories, characterized by a distorted, fragmented style.
Architect: this architect designed a 70-pointed crystal star for the annual Rockefeller Center Christmas tree ceremony.
Architect: this architect redesigned a Pittsburgh synagogue following a 2018 shooting.
